slides.headline: Your music can work in London. New York. Paris.
slides.eyebrow: Education · International markets — what UK, US, and EU actually want
fit_warnings: [01 hook.eyebrow] copy (69 chars) exceeds slot budget (48 at 44px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body: But what "works" means is different in each market. ↓

UK music supervisors are ahead on African music. They've placed it.
What they're specifically requesting:
→ Afrobeats and Afropop for lifestyle, fashion, food content
→ Highlife and Afrojuju for documentary and cultural programming
→ Contemporary orchestral work from African composers for drama
→ Roots reggae and Afro-soul for charitable campaigns
fit_warnings: [05 context.body] copy (344 chars) exceeds slot budget (206 at 48px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body:
Turnaround expectation: 24–48 hours from brief.
Rights requirement: one-stop cleared (master + sync in one conversation).
Stems: always requested — deliver without being asked. — — — THE US MARKET The US sync market is larger but harder to enter without an agent or verified track record.
What breaks through:
→ High-energy Afrobeats for sports content (NBA, NFL campaigns, streaming sports docs)
→ Minimal, emotive African acoustic for healthcare, insurance, social campaigns
→ African electronic for tech and automotive US briefs come with tighter formatting requirements.
Metadata completeness is non-negotiable — tracks with missing ISRC or split sheet fail submission. — — — THE EU MARKET European campaigns have been the earliest adopters of African music.
German and French markets specifically request:
→ African choral music for brand storytelling
→ Contemporary Afropop for consumer goods
→ African jazz for finance and luxury EU campaigns often run multi-territory.
Your sync fee should reflect it. — — — WHAT ALL THREE MARKETS REQUIRE → Full metadata: ISRC, BPM, key, mood, instrumentation, placement type
→ Stems available on request within 24 hours
→ Split sheet signed by all writers before any submission
→ Rights confirmed one-stop before any brief arrives If your catalogue isn't market-ready, international briefs won't reach you. #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #InternationalSync #SyncMaster
